purpose: Agent-maintained platform capability check. Update each entry after querying CangjieSkills/HarmonyOS/Cangjie API docs, HarmonyOS-Examples, or package index lookup for the detected capability. Use cjpm/project-management docs only to configure the chosen dependency in cjpm.toml. Direct API status requires Cangjie-callable evidence; ArkTS-only or native-only declarations should record the missing Cangjie binding as a blocker unless a target bridge/wrapper is implemented and verified.

targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-04 source inspection found no Android media-store, album, gallery picker, content URI, or runtime media permission flow in the home/history implementation surface. The actual phase-04 UI code renders bundled app resources through painterResource and R.drawable only, including presentation/history/components/HistorySection.kt:111-115 and 240-243, presentation/components/RestaurantCard.kt:70-76 and 88-89, and the static catalog media ids in data/data_source/FakeData.kt:512-803.

Current HarmonyOS Cangjie SDK does provide a direct media-library and album API through ohos.file.photo_access_helper, including getPhotoAccessHelper(context: UIAbilityContext): PhotoAccessHelper and PhotoAccessHelper.getAlbums(...), so this row is resolved as a false-positive phase match rather than missing platform support.
Phase-04 implementation confirmed the translated home/history batch still uses packaged resource images only; no additional user-media or album flow was introduced while closing the scoped rows.

Focused phase-04 verification on 2026-06-28 reran a repository-wide grep across `D:\Kotlin2Cangjie\Foodike\app\src\main\java` for `MediaStore|photo|gallery|picker|READ_MEDIA|READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE|ContentResolver|Uri|Exif|zoom` and found no matches, reinforcing that the current home/history scope has no real user-media or album-discovery flow.

Focused target verification on 2026-06-28 ran `cjpm test --target aarch64-linux-ohos` from `entry/src/test/cangjie`; `phase04_home_history_discovery_test.cj` passed while home/history surfaces continued to rely on packaged media endpoints only.

entries.capabilityName: Runtime permissions and protected platform access
entries.sourceEvidenceSummary: Detected 87 source artifact(s) with terms: permissions, permission
entries.targetStrategyHint: Map Android runtime permission flows to HarmonyOS permission APIs, preserving denied/empty/retry states.
entries.minimumParity: Request required permission, handle grant/deny, show user-facing blocked state, and avoid accessing protected APIs before authorization.
targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-02 source inspection found no Android runtime permission request or protected-API flow in the startup/session/navigation implementation scope: `presentation/MainActivity.kt`, `presentation/common/SplashViewModel.kt`, `presentation/util/Navigation.kt`, `domain/repository/HomeRepository.kt`, `domain/repository/LoginRepository.kt`, and `domain/repository/UserDataRepository.kt` contain startup routing, repository contracts, and shell behavior only.

Repository-wide source grep over `D:\Kotlin2Cangjie\Foodike\app\src\main\java` found DataStore and `Application` usage only for this phase surface; it did not find `requestPermissions`, `rememberLauncherForActivityResult`, `ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission`, `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, `WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, `POST_NOTIFICATIONS`, `Service`, `BroadcastReceiver`, `WorkManager`, or notification APIs in the inspected phase sources.

Current Cangjie SDK does provide a callable runtime-permission path through `AtManager.requestPermissionsFromUser(context: UIAbilityContext, permissionList: Array<Permissions>, requestCallback: AsyncCallback<PermissionRequestResult>)` and `PermissionRequestResult`, so this row is closed as phase-specific not-applicable rather than missing-platform-support.
entries.targetApiOrLibrary: HarmonyOS Cangjie AbilityKit permission APIs exist (`ohos.ability_access_ctrl.AtManager`, `ohos.security.permission_request_result.PermissionRequestResult`) but are not required by phase-02 startup/session/navigation source behavior.
entries.targetStrategy: Treat the phase-02 permission requirement as a source-analysis false positive. Do not add permission prompts or protected-access scaffolding during startup/session/navigation implementation unless later source-backed phases introduce real protected APIs.

entries.capabilityName: Background work, services, receivers, widgets, and notifications
entries.sourceEvidenceSummary: Detected 10 source artifact(s) with terms: notification_widget, notification, remoteviews, platform_component, service, receiver, background_work
entries.targetStrategyHint: Replace Android platform components with HarmonyOS abilities, background tasks, notifications, or documented product-level omissions.
entries.minimumParity: Preserve user-visible background behavior, notification/widget state, scheduling semantics, and lifecycle entry points when in source scope.
allowedDeferredReasons: requires signed device or service configuration
targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-02 source inspection confirmed real app-entry and lifecycle scope, but not Android background services/receivers/widgets: `FoodikeApp.kt` defines `class FoodikeApp : Application()`, `MainActivity.kt` defines a single `ComponentActivity` with `installSplashScreen().setKeepOnScreenCondition { splashViewModel.isLoading.value }`, and `SplashViewModel.kt` resolves onboarding vs home from persisted login state before normal UI rendering.

Current Cangjie SDK declares HarmonyOS app-entry and lifecycle APIs through `UIAbility`, `UIAbilityContext`, and ability startup methods such as `startAbility` / `startAbilityForResult`, which are the direct target replacement for the source `Application` + `Activity` startup surface.

HarmonyOS Cangjie navigation docs recommend ArkUI `Navigation`/`NavPathStack` or `Router` for in-app page routing, so the source single-activity navigation shell should be implemented as HarmonyOS app bootstrap plus ArkUI navigation rather than Android activity/navigation infrastructure.
entries.targetApiOrLibrary: HarmonyOS Cangjie AbilityKit app-entry APIs (`ohos.app.ability.ui_ability.UIAbility`, `UIAbilityContext`, `Want`) plus ArkUI `Navigation`/`NavPathStack` or `Router` for in-app startup and route flow
entries.targetStrategy: Resolve the source Android platform component surface as a HarmonyOS platform replacement: model app startup in the target `UIAbility`/descriptor entry, keep splash-like gating inside bootstrap state before rendering the main route, and implement onboarding/home navigation using the existing ArkUI route shell instead of Android `Application`, `ComponentActivity`, and `core-splashscreen` APIs.
